proof_sketch: The kernel accumulates A[i][k] * B[k][j] for k=0..K-1 in strictly increasing order. The outer loop over k_tile and inner loop over k from 0 to TILE_K-1 ensures that for each fixed (i,j), the products are added in exactly the same sequence regardless of TILE_K. Changing TILE_K from 8 to 7 does not alter the order of individual multiply-add operations; it only changes the grouping of loads into shared memory, which does not affect arithmetic. Boundary handling (zero-fill) preserves correctness. Therefore the floating-point result is bitwise identical for all inputs.
